strategy_criteria_summary: USA-listed companies reporting earnings next week, sorted by market cap. This deliberately broad Finviz screen preserves the top five visible rows; optionability, liquidity, trend, news, fundamentals, and contract quality are validated downstream.
validation_notes: market cap differs across sources; kept Finviz ranking value
scoring_reasons: relative strength was clearly supportive|trend alignment was mixed rather than decisive|news/catalyst quality was mixed rather than decisive|best contract was long_put 2026-05-15 195|Spread is wider than ideal but still usable.
decision_reasoning: MP offers the cleanest long-only setup in the slate: strong multi-timeframe trend (+28% 20d, +18% 50d), clear relative strength vs SPY (+20.7%) and sector (+28.8%), above-average volume, and a fresh Wedbush Outperform with a $90 PT providing a bullish narrative into the 05-07 print. Data confidence is high (93). The 71-strike 05-08 call has the best liquidity and tightest spread on the chain with a manageable ~4.9% breakeven. Score lands in the Standard recommend band rather than Strong because news flow is genuinely two-sided (analysts flag lack of earnings-beat indicators, Cramer skeptical on valuation), earnings timing is 'unknown' so BMO/AMC fit is inferred, and IV/delta are not reported for the chosen strike. IONQ has stronger momentum but IV ~1.14 presents serious crush risk on a long call through earnings; CSCO's best contract requires a 9%+ move to breakeven; SPG is a put (trend mixed, thin liquidity) and weaker overall.
